anchor.prefix: ssing the ground. Too fast → it escapes.
anchor.quote: Set the muzzle speed and hit Fire. Too slow and it crashes back down; hit the sweet spot and it settles into a closed orbit; push harder and it swings out into an ellipse, then escapes altogether. Same fall, different sideways speed.

comments.body: The NEWTON’s CANNONBALL Animation doesn’t seem to work. Can you have a look.
comments.reply: Fixed the cannonball physics. The bug: muzzle speed was a fixed pixel velocity unrelated to the planet's gravity, and its maximum sat below the circular-orbit speed, so no setting could ever orbit — the ball always fell back. Rewrote the muzzle to launch at a fraction of the local circular velocity (scale-independent) and smoothed the integrator (symplectic Euler, smaller sub-steps) so orbits close. Now slider ≲35% falls back, ~45–70% settles into a closed orbit (default 55% traces a clean ellipse), and ≲86% escapes — matching the widget's own caption. Verified from file://.
